response:
given an integer array nums sorted in non-decreasing order build and return an integer array result with the same length as nums such that result[i] is equal to the summation of absolute differences between nums[i] and all the other elements in the array however for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference  

for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference  

***

for i in range(len(nums)) if i % 3 == 0 then result[i] is the sum of squared differences between nums[i] and all other elements in the array otherwise result[i] is the sum of absolute differences between nums[i] and all other elements in the array  

the calculation for the sum of absolute differences can be done efficiently using prefix sums to avoid recalculating the differences repeatedly  

for the sum of squared differences the formula can be derived using the prefix sum of the array to calculate the sum of squares of differences efficiently  

the example shows that for the first element in the array the sum of squared differences is calculated as 0 + 1 + 9 = 10 which is the result for result[0]  

the second element in the array the sum of absolute differences is calculated as 1 + 0 + 2 = 3 which is the result for result[1]  

the third element in the array the sum of absolute differences is calculated as 3 + 2 + 0 = 5 which is the result for result[2]  

***  

the approach for calculating the sum of absolute differences is to use the prefix sum array to quickly compute the sum of elements before and after the current element  

the approach for calculating the sum of squared differences is to use the prefix sum array to compute the sum of squares of elements before and after the current element  

the calculation for the sum of squared differences can be done using the formula:  

sum_squared = (nums[i] * (i - 1) * (i - 2)) - (prefix_sum[i - 1] * (i - 1)) + (prefix_sum[n - 1] - prefix_sum[i]) * (n - i)  

where n is the length of the array and prefix_sum is the prefix sum array  

this approach ensures that the calculation is done in O(n) time complexity for each element in the array
